content: I do research in multi-party secure computation, with a specific focus on developing specialized protocols that enhance efficiency across various secure computation problems, such as Private Set Intersection (PSI), secure inference in the context of machine learning, and secure aggregation, etc. My goal is to integrate privacy-preserving technology more effectively into real-world industries.
summary: I do research in multi-party secure computation, with a specific focus on developing specialized protocols that enhance efficiency across various secure computation problems, such as Private Set Intersection (PSI), secure inference in the context of machine learning, and secure aggregation, etc.
title: Nan Cheng

content: Supplementary notes can be added here, including code and math.
summary: The two-party private set union/intersection cardinality problems (PSI/PSU-CA) are among the privacy-preserving computing problems, which are also fundamental and essential. They require that after protocol execution, at least one of the two parties gets their intersection/union cardinality securely with neither one leaked any other extra information to the other.
title: An efficient approach regarding two party private set union/intersection cardinality
